summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue type in the ars-ui Rust component library, delivered as a single PR closing GitHub issues #297, #298, and #299.
summary.outcome: All 8 components plus ars-core color foundation were implemented, tested (4074+ lib tests, 5 proptests, 106 conformance), and merged into PR #698. Two rounds of Codex review findings (14 total) were addressed TDD-first and pushed; CI is green on all 22 checks. A third Codex pass was triggered but not yet resolved at transcript end.

repo: core_maths (v0.1, default-features=false) is the workspace's established no_std float tool; methods must be called in fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x) as ars-i18n does, not as inherent methods
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max — the workspace pattern is to add private fmin/fmax helpers that use bit-casting comparisons
MessageFn uses hand-written From<F> impls per closure signature rather than a blanket generic; new signatures require explicit additions to message_fn.rs
CssProperty::Custom renders as --{name}, so the name arg must omit the leading -- (spec had double-dashes)
cargo xci-fast has a snapshot-count gate requiring ≥1 insta snapshot per declared state variant — AngleSlider's 3 states needed 3 snapshots
spec-compile-snippets compiles spec code blocks in isolation; spec drift causes no immediate CI failure but the blocks must be internally consistent
CI native coverage uses cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace; the --workspace flag causes cross-crate tests to boost ars-components coverage ~1.1% vs -p measurement alone
ars-components threshold is 90% line AND 90% branch; one-armed if flag {} conditionals need both arms covered to satisfy branch threshold
The workspace deny-list includes clippy::suspicious, meaning newly triggered pre-existing lints on a new stable toolchain version break CI for all code
Bindable<T>::get() returns the controlled prop in controlled mode; the just-dragged internal value is only accessible via the new Bindable::pending() method added during this session
Service::new panics on empty Props::id — test helpers must inject a non-empty fallback id
Effects are not executed by Service::send; they are drained from result.pending_effects and run via effect.run(svc.context(), svc.props(), send)
track_pointer_drag exists in ars-core but is unused by any component; use_platform_effects() does not exist — the actual adapter-driven drag pattern mirrors input/slider
AngleSlider Part::Marker { value: f64 } cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 is not Eq/Hash; a manual impl using to_bits() is required (same pattern as input/slider's track-position Part)

Codex (chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]) uses a reaction-based lifecycle: posts 👀 while reviewing, replaces with 👍 on approval or drops 👀 and posts threads on findings; rate limits are per-account quota, not per-PR

on_props_changed + SyncValue/SetProps is the workspace pattern for controlled prop sync; without it, controlled value/disabled/readonly go stale until remount — none of the 6 stateful color machines had it initially

code.finding: from_hex must guard with hex.is_ascii() before byte-indexing — non-ASCII input (e.g. #ああ) is 6 bytes but contains multi-byte chars, causing a panic at a non-char boundary before from_str_radix
parse_rgb_args/parse_hsl_args/parse_hsb_args: parse::<f64>() succeeds for NaN and inf; must validate is_finite() and return None — added parse_finite_f64 helper used at all numeric parse sites

Each distinct closure signature needs its own From<F> impl block; added 4 color-specific ones: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(f64,f64,&str,&str,&Locale); later rounds added Fn(&str,&str,&Locale) for slider valuetext and Fn(&str,&str,&str,&Locale) for area valuetext

Added Bindable::pending() -> &T returning the internal (last set()) value; needed so drag on_change_end callbacks report the just-dragged value in controlled mode rather than the stale controlled prop

channel_is_percentage helper identifies saturation/lightness/brightness/alpha channels; format_value multiplies by 100 for display, commit_input divides by 100 before clamping — without this, blur on a 50% saturation field committed 1.0 (100%) instead of 0.5
SetProps must reformat ctx.input_text using the new channel/format when the field is not focused — otherwise the display text stays in the old format after a programmatic channel switch

Readonly must set HtmlAttr::ReadOnly on the native input AND block Change + blur commit_input in the machine transition; aria-readonly alone does not prevent form submission or programmatic value changes
tabbable_index() returns selected index or 0 as fallback; without it, all items get tabindex=-1 in idle state making the control unreachable by keyboard Tab — Focus event was therefore unreachable

format_axis_reading(channel, value) produces channel-aware readings (hue in degrees, fractional in %, rgb raw); value_text previously multiplied all channels by 100 causing hue x-channel to announce '18000%' instead of '180°'
Hue wrap must use rem_euclid(360.0) not %; with large_step > 360 the % operator returns a negative remainder, storing negative hue values in context

Pre-existing map_or_else(.., identity) pattern trips clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else on stable 1.96 (released 3 days into this session); workspace denies clippy::suspicious so this blocked xci-fast — fixed incidentally as an unblock

workflow: TDD was the required pattern for all Codex review findings: write a failing test, implement the fix, confirm the test passes before moving to the next finding
Spec reconciliation is mandatory after any contract change (new events, new method signatures, behavioral changes); cargo xtask spec validate must pass before push
cargo xci-fast (10 gates) must pass before every push; the snapshot-count gate is the trickiest — it counts state variants from the derive macro and requires one snapshot each
Branch coverage requires explicit both-arms tests for every if flag {} conditional in connect/attrs methods; the CI --workspace flag makes the crate-level number ~1.1% higher than -p measurement
When Codex is rate-limited, polling for 👍 is pointless — stop the loop and wait for quota reset rather than burning time on repeated @codex review triggers
Spec code blocks may describe non-existent APIs (the specs predated the impl); reconciling spec to implementation (not the other way) is the correct direction when APIs don't exist in the codebase
For large cross-cutting tasks (same fix in 4-6 files), fixing one file fully and verifying before replicating prevents compounding errors across related machines
The waiting-for-codex-review skill manages the full 👀/👍 lifecycle including reply-and-resolve of threads; it should be re-triggered after each push to a round with open threads

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max — required discovering the gap at compile time and writing fmin/fmax helpers; the CoreFloat docs/README don't list the missing methods

Spec described use_platform_effects() and track_pointer_drag() in core as the drag pattern — neither exists; required investigating all existing drag components to derive the real adapter-driven pattern
Pre-existing pagination/mod.rs clippy lint surfaced on stable 1.96 (released mid-session), blocking cargo xci-fast on unrelated code before the work could be pushed
Codex rate-limited after the initial review trigger ('You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews') — the review loop was blocked for an unknown duration
CI Coverage gate failed post-push (89.7% branch < 90% threshold); required a follow-up commit adding both-arms tests and discovering a real dead-branch bug (has_description had no setter)
Background watch script crashed due to zsh treating 'status' as a read-only special variable — the script had to be rewritten with a different variable name
TransitionPlan closure type inference (E0282) cascaded through every early-return closure in color_field's machine, requiring manual |ctx: &mut Context| annotations on each
color_area F6 valuetext format changed the thumb snapshot, requiring an explicit insta snapshot accept step mid-fix rather than being auto-accepted
AngleSlider snapshot-count gate failure discovered only after the initial xci-fast run, requiring an additional fix commit before push
Spec code blocks are rust-fenced but cross-reference symbols from other blocks — they can't be compiled as-is, making spec validation a parse/structure check rather than a compilation check
open_items: Codex pass 3 was triggered at transcript end but its verdict is unknown — the review loop must resume and run until 👍 before merge
Merge of PR #698 is the user's decision; issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge via keywords in the PR body
ColorValue could be promoted from the color-picker spec to spec/shared/color-types.md — deferred because it touches manifest + 8 spec frontmatters and is orthogonal to correctness
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*.md) describe the framework-specific layer for all 8 components — explicitly out of scope per the issues, future task

spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md representative MessageFn From-impl listing was not updated with the 4 new color-specific signatures — left inconsistent because adding only the new area signature while sibling color impls remain unlisted would be worse than leaving it as-is
The transitive-dep block v0.1.6 future-incompat warning in the workspace is pre-existing and unresolved

The color swatch picker's navigation-while-focused events (FocusUp/FocusDown/FocusFirst/FocusLast) have no corresponding test for wrap-around edge cases — covered by proptest but not by named unit tests
